key_drivers: Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruption risk are keeping oil-supply and geopolitical-risk inputs firmly supportive for crude and defensive FX.
FedWatch-linked reporting indicates markets see no U.S. rate cut this year, while U.S. real yields have risen on the latest FRED print, creating a headwind for duration-sensitive assets.

Policy divergence remains constructive for non-USD FX: the BoJ kept the overnight call rate around 0.75% with inflation expectations still elevated, while recent ECB and RBA communication remains non-dovish.
China's latest private manufacturing PMI signaled expansion, helping cushion Australia-linked growth inputs even as broader risk sentiment remains fragile.

overnight_changes: Front-month WTI crude rose to about 114.69 from 112.41
Copper slipped marginally to about 5.582 from 5.5835

background: Middle East conflict developments around Iran and the Strait of Hormuz
China growth follow-through after March PMI improvement
